Chart of Accounts

The chart of accounts is your organisation's complete list of general ledger accounts. Every transaction in Merlin — posted purchase invoices, recorded payments, sales invoice income — is assigned to one or more accounts from this chart. A default chart is seeded on installation and can be extended or modified to fit your organisation's needs.

Account structure

Accounts are organised in a three-level hierarchy:

  1. Account type — the top-level classification: Asset, Liability, Equity, Income/Revenue, or Expense. Account types have a normal balance (debit or credit) and are fixed in the application.
  2. Account group — a container within an account type, such as "Current Assets" or "Current Liabilities". Account groups organise accounts for reporting purposes. See Account Groups.
  3. Account — the individual ledger account where transactions are posted. Accounts can also be nested within a parent account for further sub-grouping.

Account fields

FieldRequiredNotes
Account groupYesWhich group this account belongs to
Parent accountNoOptional parent account for sub-accounts; must be an existing account
CodeYesShort account code, e.g. 5010; max 20 characters
NameYesDescriptive account name, e.g. "Cloud Hosting"; max 255 characters
DescriptionNoOptional longer explanation; max 1 000 characters
ActiveYesInactive accounts are hidden from selection menus but retained in reports
Allow direct postingYesWhether transactions can be posted directly to this account (see below)

Direct posting

The allow_direct_posting flag controls whether an account can receive individual transaction postings. Parent or summary accounts (used for grouping sub-accounts) should have this turned off so that all transactions flow to the leaf-level accounts. Accounts with direct posting disabled will not appear in account selection menus on invoices or line items.

System accounts

Accounts marked is_system = true are protected accounts created by the application for internal use (such as the accounts payable control account). NEEDS CONFIRMATION: exact UI restrictions on system accounts — whether they can be renamed or deactivated.

The seeded chart

The default chart seeded by ChartOfAccountsSeeder includes the following illustrative structure (your actual codes may vary):

Code rangeAccount typeExample groups and accounts
1000sAssetCurrent Assets (Bank, Accounts Receivable)
2000sLiabilityCurrent Liabilities (Accounts Payable at 2000, GST/VAT payable)
3000sEquityEquity (Retained earnings, owner contributions)
4000sIncomeRevenue (Sales, service income)
5000sExpenseExpenses (Cloud hosting, communication, professional services, and others)

These accounts are illustrative. You can rename, add to, or deactivate any seeded account through the chart of accounts UI at any time.

Permissions

  • accounts-view-any / accounts-view — to view the chart
  • accounts-create — to add new accounts
  • accounts-update — to edit existing accounts
  • accounts-delete — to soft-delete accounts
  • accounts-restore / accounts-force-delete — to restore or permanently delete accounts
  • account-types-view-any — to view account types (required for the account group form)
